title: Neottia smallii
taxonomy: Neottia smallii is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is placed within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. Additionally, it is classified in the genus Neottia, which is a group of orchids commonly known as the "bee orchids" due to their resemblance to bees. Neottia smallii is native to the southeastern United States, where it grows in woodland environments. The exact classification of Neottia smallii may be subject to revision as new research and discoveries are made in the field of botany.
uses: Neottia smallii, also known as the Appalachian ladies' tresses, has been traditionally used in medicine due to its various properties. Its roots have been used in herbal remedies to treat a range of conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe wounds and reduce swelling. Additionally, Neottia smallii has been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. However, further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and confirm its efficacy in treating these conditions.
benefits: Neottia smallii is a rare orchid species with potential health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effects.
